workflow.name: Failure Replay Lab
workflow.tagline: Debug a failed MCP tool step without raw log archaeology
workflow.description: Watch a browser MCP step fail, preserve the trace, and inspect the replay artifact.

data.label: Bug Report
data.description: A user reports that checkout fails after a UI change.

data.label: Triage Agent
data.description: Build a local hypothesis before touching tools.

data.label: Browser Replay
data.description: Simulated browser MCP timeout with preserved replay context.

data.label: Failure Artifact
data.description: Simulate capturing screenshot, command, stderr, and prior prompt context.

data.label: Fix Plan
data.description: Produce a replayable failure report and next action.

portableWorkflow.name: Failure Replay Lab
portableWorkflow.description: Watch a browser MCP step fail, preserve the trace, and inspect the replay artifact.

trace.summary: Bug Report normalized the checkout failure into success criteria and constraints.

artifacts.name: Bug Report notes
artifacts.content:
# Bug Report

Checkout started failing after a UI change. The run should identify the failing browser/MCP step and preserve evidence.

debug.prompt: A user reports that checkout fails after a UI change.
debug.result: Failure criteria captured for replay debugging.
debug.stdout:
Bug Report: normalized context
Bug Report: complete

trace.inputPreview: Checkout fails after a UI change.
trace.outputPreview: Browser replay should verify checkout and preserve failure evidence.

trace.summary: Triage Agent built a local hypothesis and selected the browser replay tool.

artifacts.name: Triage Agent payload

debug.prompt: Build a local hypothesis before touching tools.
debug.result: Use browser replay to reproduce checkout and capture artifacts.

trace.outputPreview: Browser replay selected as the next debugging step.

trace.summary: Browser Replay failed with a simulated MCP timeout for replay debugging.

artifacts.name: Browser Replay notes
artifacts.content:
# Browser Replay

The MCP browser step timed out while waiting for the checkout submit button.

artifacts.name: Browser Replay stdout
artifacts.content:
Browser Replay: start
Browser Replay: opening /checkout
Browser Replay: waiting for checkout submit

artifacts.name: Browser Replay stderr
artifacts.content: DEMO_TOOL_TIMEOUT: browser MCP timed out waiting for button[data-testid=checkout-submit]

artifacts.name: Browser Replay screenshot
artifacts.content: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 640 360" role="img"><title>Browser Replay timeout screenshot</title><rect width="640" height="360" fill="#eff6ff"/><rect x="36" y="42" width="568" height="276" rx="8" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#bfdbfe"/><rect x="72" y="92" width="210" height="44" rx="6" fill="#dbeafe"/><rect x="72" y="198" width="300" height="22" rx="4" fill="#fee2e2"/><circle cx="520" cy="118" r="34" fill="#e11d48" opacity="0.12"/><path d="M506 118h28" stroke="#e11d48" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/><text x="72" y="258" fill="#0f172a" font-family="Arial" font-size="22" font-weight="700">Checkout button not found</text></svg>

debug.prompt:
Workflow: Failure Replay Lab
Step 3: Browser Replay
Goal: Watch a browser MCP step fail, preserve the trace, and inspect the replay artifact.
debug.result: Browser Replay timed out in the simulated replay harness.
debug.stdout:
Browser Replay: start
Browser Replay: normalized context
Browser Replay: failed
debug.stderr: DEMO_TOOL_TIMEOUT: browser MCP timed out waiting for button[data-testid=checkout-submit]

trace.inputPreview: Use browser MCP to replay checkout and click submit.

error.message: This demo failure keeps the graph, trace, cost, stderr, screenshot, and tool payload inspectable after the failed step.

trace.summary: Browser Replay replayed with preserved prompt, tool call, and artifact context.

artifacts.name: Replay result: Browser Replay notes
artifacts.content:
# Replay Result

The failed MCP step was replayed with the original prompt, tool call, stderr, and screenshot attached.

debug.prompt: Replay Browser Replay with preserved failure context.
debug.result: Replay attached the original failure evidence and produced a shareable debugging note.

trace.outputPreview: Replay evidence bundle is ready to export.
